basics.title: Z/L-Target, then circle
basics.body: Press L (GameCube) or ZL/L (Wii U HD) to lock onto the nearest enemy — the camera fixes on it and Link keeps facing it while you sidestep. Locked on, you can roll around a foe to its back, do a jumping side-strike, or hop straight back to dodge. Untargeted swinging is for crowds; for any single dangerous enemy, target first and learn its rhythm.

basics.title: The Parry counter — your opening button
basics.body: While you're Z/L-targeting an enemy and an A-button prompt flashes (Link's sword glints) just as the foe is about to strike, hit A for a Parry: Link rolls behind or vaults over the enemy and counters. This is the heart of Wind Waker combat. A clean parry instantly kills weak foes like Bokoblins, and against tougher enemies it creates the opening — on a Darknut each parry knocks off a piece of armor (it does NOT kill from behind in one hit). Watch for the flash and tap A the instant it appears.

basics.title: Steal a weapon for reach and power
basics.body: Knock a Bokoblin or Moblin's weapon out of its hands (a few hits, or it drops one when stunned) and stand over the loose weapon to pick it up with A. Boko sticks, Moblin spears, and Darknut swords give Link far more reach than his sword — a Moblin's spear in particular lets you poke enemies from a safe distance. Borrowed weapons break or are dropped over time, but grabbing one mid-fight can turn a tough group fast.

basics.title: Deku Leaf + the Magic meter
basics.body: The Deku Leaf (won at the Forest Haven, before you enter the Forbidden Woods) does two jobs and both drain the green Magic meter: swing it as a melee gust to shove light enemies (Bokoblins, Boko Babas) off ledges or to flip switches, and hold it open after a jump to glide on rising gusts. Magic refills from green jars and grass, and the meter can be doubled later by a Great Fairy. Don't burn it all gliding if a fight's coming.

basics.title: Bottled Fairies, soup, and healing
basics.body: Carry a Fairy in a bottle and it works as an auto-revive — if Link's hearts hit zero, the Fairy bursts out and restores eight hearts instead of a Game Over, so always keep one bottled before a boss. Touching a bottled Fairy below full health also heals eight hearts. Grandma's Elixir Soup (after you cure her) heals all hearts AND magic and doubles your attack power until you take a hit — each bottle holds two servings. Otherwise grab dropped hearts or refill bottles at fairy fountains.

basics.title: Sailing combat and the boat's cannon
basics.body: At sea you fight from the King of Red Lions. Airborne pests like Seahats you knock down with the Boomerang or arrows; sea enemies and gun-platforms you blow up with the boat's Cannon (aim with the reticle and fire). A Big Octo is the deep-sea exception — its whirlpool drags you in and you must hit every one of its eyes (Bow, Cannon, or Boomerang) before you reach the center. Mind the wind: you can't sail against it, so set your heading with the Wind Waker's 'Wind's Requiem' before a chase.

enemies.name: Bokoblin
enemies.tactic: The basic grunt — a couple of sword slashes, or wait for the A-prompt and Parry to drop it in one move. Knock its stick or sword loose and steal it for extra reach.
enemies.drops: Sometimes its weapon; nothing else notable

enemies.name: Bokoblin Archer
enemies.tactic: Bokoblins posted with bows snipe from walls and towers, especially in the Forsaken Fortress. Close the gap fast (roll toward them) or pick them off first with your own bow or the Boomerang so they don't chip you while you fight melee foes.

enemies.name: Moblin
enemies.tactic: Big pig-faced brute with a spear and much more reach than a Bokoblin. Target it, bait a thrust, then parry or roll behind to strike — or steal its spear, one of the best reach weapons in the game. Several patrol the Forsaken Fortress.

enemies.name: Miniblin
enemies.tactic: Tiny imps that swarm in endless numbers, jabbing with little tridents and shrieking 'Kee-kee!'. Individually trivial (one hit each) but the danger is being mobbed — keep moving, sweep with spin attacks, and don't get cornered.

enemies.name: ChuChu
enemies.tactic: Gelatinous blobs in several colors: Green and Red are standard (a slash or two kills them; Green can melt into a puddle to dodge). Blue and Yellow are electrified — don't slash them directly; stun first with the Boomerang or an arrow, then finish. Yellow ones also hold arrows.
enemies.drops: Chu Jelly (Green/Red/Blue by color); Yellow drops Red Jelly + arrows. Take jelly to Doc Bandam on Windfall to brew potions

enemies.name: Keese
enemies.tactic: Small bats that flutter erratically and dive at Link, sometimes carrying fire or ice. A single sword swing, a Boomerang toss, or an arrow drops one. Clear them before they distract you during a bigger fight; targeting makes the swing connect.

enemies.name: Peahat
enemies.tactic: Floating spinning plant-creatures with a leafy top. While the blades whirl they're protected; wait for the spinning to stop (or hit them with the Boomerang/arrows), then slash. The Deku Leaf's gust can also knock them around.

enemies.name: Boko Baba
enemies.tactic: Carnivorous plants on stalks in the Forbidden Woods that snap at Link. Slash the bulb to kill it; cutting one yields a Boko Baba Seed, and the stalk can leave a usable Bokoblin-style stick.
enemies.drops: Boko Baba Seed; sometimes a stick

enemies.name: Magtail
enemies.tactic: Armored centipede-like crawlers found in fiery caverns, notably Dragon Roost Cavern. Their shell shrugs off sword hits — strike the eye when they rear up to attack, or hit the eye with the Grappling Hook to make them curl into a ball. Curled, they can be picked up and set on switches.

enemies.name: Wizzrobe
enemies.tactic: Robed casters that teleport in, summon other monsters, and fire magic before vanishing. Target one and rush it the instant it appears — a couple of hits banish it. Killing the Wizzrobe stops the reinforcements it keeps spawning, so prioritize it.

enemies.name: Stalfos
enemies.tactic: Reanimated skeletons that block and counter. Parry when prompted to knock them apart, then — important — they reassemble unless you destroy the scattered skull. Smash or pick up the bouncing skull before it pulls the bones back together.

enemies.name: ReDead
enemies.tactic: Mummified horrors in dark dungeons (notably the Earth Temple) that freeze Link with a scream, then climb on and bite. If it shrieks, mash buttons and roll the stick to break free, or pre-empt it: sneak up and hit it before it screams, or use Light Arrows to wipe it instantly.

enemies.name: Floormaster
enemies.tactic: Pale crawling hands that drop from the ceiling and grab Link — if one grabs you it hauls you a short way back (often to the room's entrance). Slash it quickly; a hit splits it into three small hands, so kill those fast before they regroup into a full one.

enemies.name: Seahat
enemies.tactic: Flying ray-like sea pests that buzz the King of Red Lions and dive at you while sailing. Knock them down with the Boomerang or an arrow, or blast them with the boat's Cannon before they reach you. Annoying in groups but individually weak.

enemies.name: Darknut
enemies.tactic: Heavily armored knights — the toughest standard enemy. You can't hurt the armored body, so Parry each time it attacks to knock off pieces of armor, then attack the exposed warrior underneath.
enemies.drops: Sometimes drops its sword (a strong reach weapon)
enemies.battle: Bring full hearts and ideally a bottled Fairy; the Master Sword and patience are enough. Darknuts have a sword and shield and block everything head-on, so never trade blows. Z/L-target and watch for the A-prompt: each clean Parry strips off a piece of its armor (cape/back plate first, then the rest) — parrying does NOT kill it from behind in one hit, it un-armors it. You can also slash its armor to wear pieces off, and knocking off back armor passes that damage to its health. Once it's stripped to its undershirt it dies in a few hits but moves faster and more aggressively, so keep rolling and back-hopping its heavy swings. The Boomerang can stun it for free stabs, and you can grab its dropped sword for reach. Take them one at a time when several appear.

enemies.name: Big Octo
enemies.tactic: Enormous whirlpool-octopus encountered at sea (find it by a ring of seagulls); its current drags your boat toward the middle. Defeat it by hitting every one of its eyes before you reach the center.
enemies.battle: Have the Bow with plenty of arrows (the Cannon and Boomerang also hit the eyes — the Cannon and Boomerang kill an eye in one shot, an arrow takes three hits per eye). Big Octos sit at fixed spots on the Great Sea and come in 4-eye, 8-eye, and 12-eye sizes. When the swirling current grabs the King of Red Lions, ready your weapon and clear every glowing eye on all sides before you're pulled to the center — if you fail it spits you to the edge of the sea sector and you start over. Clearing one rewards, by size, the freed Great Fairy doubling your Magic meter (the 4-eye one), 100 Rupees, or a Piece of Heart — fished up afterward with the crane. It's an aiming test, not a melee fight; carry spare arrows so you don't run dry mid-whirlpool.

enemies.name: Gohma
enemies.tactic: Armored arthropod boss of Dragon Roost Cavern, hanging beneath Valoo's tail. Drop the ceiling on it with the Grappling Hook to crack its shell, then attack its exposed eye.
enemies.battle: Bring the Grappling Hook (the dungeon item) and your sword; full hearts help. Gohma's body is sheathed in tough armor, so don't bother swinging at it at first. When Gohma traps you in its claws and inhales, Z/L-target Valoo's glowing tail dangling from the ceiling, fire the Grappling Hook to grab it, and swing — a sheet of rock crashes down onto Gohma and cracks its shell. Do this three times to shatter the armor and expose the big eye. Now target the eye and fire the Grappling Hook at it to reel Gohma in and stun it, then slash the eye repeatedly with your sword. Repeat the eye phase a couple of times and Gohma explodes, granting a Heart Container. Land on the upper wooden platforms between hits — Gohma can't reach you there, though it will try to burn them.

enemies.name: Kalle Demos
enemies.tactic: Giant carnivorous plant (a huge Boko Baba) of the Forbidden Woods, hanging from the ceiling by vine-tentacles that hide its flower core. Cut the vines to drop it, then strike the exposed bulb.
enemies.battle: Bring the Boomerang (the dungeon item) plus your sword; the Grappling Hook can also sever vines. Kalle Demos dangles from green tentacles anchoring it to the ceiling while barbed vines pop from the floor — keep moving so they can't whip you (they glow just before they strike). Use the Boomerang to slice the ceiling-vines: lock onto several at once to cut multiple per throw. Once the anchor vines are cut the plant crashes down and its red flower core opens — rush in and slash the core hard, then back out before the petals snap shut (that bite hurts a lot). It re-anchors and the cycle repeats. Secret one-shot: if you bottled Forest Water from the Forest Haven, pour it on Kalle Demos after dropping it to kill it instantly. Win for a Heart Container.

enemies.name: Gohdan
enemies.tactic: Stone-idol guardian of the Tower of the Gods — a floating head flanked by two giant hands. Shoot the hands and the eyes with the Bow, then bomb the open mouth.
enemies.battle: Bring the Bow and Bombs (both found in the Tower of the Gods). Each of Gohdan's two hands has an eye — shoot each hand-eye twice with the Bow to make that hand go limp so it can no longer punch, grab, or shove you off the platform. With both hands down, Gohdan's two head-eyes open; shoot each of those twice as well. The head then slumps to the floor with its mouth gaping — throw a Bomb straight into the mouth. Repeat the whole cycle (hands, eyes, bomb) three times total to win. Dodge its fireballs by running, and if you run out of arrows Gohdan conveniently sneezes out ten more, so you'll never be stranded. Rewards a Heart Container and the Master Sword chamber below.

enemies.name: Helmaroc King
enemies.tactic: The huge masked bird that kidnapped Aryll, fought atop the Forsaken Fortress. Its iron mask blocks your sword — crack it with the Skull Hammer, then finish with the blade.
enemies.battle: You need the Skull Hammer (won earlier on this return trip) plus the Master Sword and full hearts; bottle a Fairy first. Phase one is a chase up the tower's spiral ramp as water rises and the bird smashes the stairs — roll to move fast and jump or Grappling-Hook the gaps; at the top the Helmaroc shoves its head through the doorway, so Skull-Hammer its mask to send it plummeting. On the rooftop, bait its diving beak peck by strafing at medium range, then dodge so the beak sticks in the floor. Run up and slam the mask with the Skull Hammer — about four or five good blows shatter it. With the mask broken the exposed head is now vulnerable to the Master Sword (and Hammer/arrows); Z/L-target and slash to finish it. If it hovers and kicks up a gale, move toward it or perpendicular to the wind, not away. Victory yields a Heart Container.

enemies.name: Jalhalla
enemies.tactic: Colossal Poe boss of the Earth Temple, made of many smaller Poes. Pin it with reflected sunlight using the Mirror Shield, then hurl its solid body into the wall spikes.
enemies.battle: You need the Mirror Shield (the Earth Temple item) and the Power Bracelets; the sword finishes the job. Jalhalla is intangible until lit — stand in a column of sunlight pouring into the room, raise the Mirror Shield, and angle the beam onto it. Held in the light, the giant Poe solidifies and drops. Immediately lift it with the Power Bracelets and throw it into one of the spike-columns on the walls; it bursts into a swarm of small Poes. Draw your sword and cut down as many as you can (one or two hits each) — but only about five can be killed per cycle, and if even one Poe survives it reforms the whole boss. Repeat: light it, throw it, slash the Poes. Late in the fight the sunlight pillars start vanishing, so move quickly to keep a beam on it. Defeat it for a Heart Container.

enemies.name: Molgera
enemies.tactic: Giant sandworm boss of the Wind Temple that burrows through the sand floor. Hookshot its exposed tongue to yank it down, then sword the tongue.
enemies.battle: Bring the Hookshot (the Wind Temple item) and your sword, with full hearts. Watch the sand for a dark shifting patch — that's where Molgera will surface. When it pops up with its mouth gaping and the red-purple tongue showing, Z/L-target the tongue and fire the Hookshot at it to drag the beast down to your level, then slash the tongue rapidly with the Master Sword. Don't linger right in front of the open mouth or it'll suck you in and swallow you for a couple hearts of damage. After it's hurt it spawns three Molgera larvae — Hookshot and cut each out of the sand (they drop hearts and magic), or stay on the boss. Also watch for Molgera leaping airborne to crash down on you; roll aside when it flies. About three full tongue-Hookshot-and-slash cycles finishes it, granting a Heart Container.

enemies.name: Puppet Ganon
enemies.tactic: A marionette of Ganon fought in Ganon's Tower, in three escalating forms (puppet, spider, snake). Each form has a glowing orb weak point you destroy with Light Arrows.
enemies.battle: You must have the Light Arrows (given by Zelda before this fight) plus the Boomerang and Bombs. Form 1 — Marionette: it dangles from strings; use the Boomerang to cut the strings (especially the one holding up the tail), and once the tail drops, Light-Arrow the orb on the tail. It re-strings itself and you repeat — three orb hits total. Form 2 — Spider (Bug-Ganon): it climbs the ceiling and crashes down on your spot; use the shallow water on the floor as a mirror to read where it'll land, dodge clear, then Light-Arrow the blue orb on its underside, three times. Form 3 — Snake/Worm: it slithers fast and erratically, speeding up each time you hit the tail orb; stun it first — a Bomb to the head, or a Light Arrow/sword hit to the face makes it pause — then quickly Light-Arrow the tail-tip orb. Down all three forms to clear the puppet and proceed to Ganondorf.

enemies.name: Ganondorf
enemies.tactic: The true final boss, dueled atop Ganon's Tower alongside Princess Zelda. He blocks normal attacks — you win through Parries, Zelda's Light Arrows, and finally a reflected Light Arrow off the Mirror Shield.
enemies.battle: Bring the Master Sword (re-powered by the sages Medli and Makar), the Mirror Shield, full hearts, and a bottled Fairy. Early on, Zelda fires Light Arrows at Ganondorf while you fight; you open him up by Parrying his sword attacks (or pressuring him until his guard drops to trigger a parry), then unload with the Master Sword — and a Light Arrow that connects stuns him the same way, so let Zelda create openings. After Zelda is knocked out and revived, she asks you to deflect her Light Arrows: catch one on the Mirror Shield and bounce it into Ganondorf. The reflected arrow paralyzes him — run up, and at the Finishing Blow prompt Link leaps and drives the Master Sword into Ganondorf's forehead, turning him to stone. Keep the Fairy in reserve for his big combos.
